frameworks.name: rate code
frameworks.one_line: treats firing rate as the neural observable carrying stimulus information

frameworks.name: spike-timing code
frameworks.one_line: treats precise spike times as the neural observable carrying information

frameworks.name: downstream-readout perspective
frameworks.one_line: information code is defined by how postsynaptic neurons integrate inputs

core_question: Is the rate-versus-spike dichotomy the right way to ask how the brain encodes information?
key_assumptions: what counts as 'the code' depends on who is reading it
downstream neurons have characteristic temporal integration windows
synaptic dynamics shape effective readout timescale
manipulations: shift of analytic frame from external-observer to downstream-readout
consideration of varying readout integration windows and synaptic dynamics
key_findings: the external-observer framing obscures the empirically relevant question
rate-vs-spike is a continuum, not a dichotomy, once readout is specified
the position on that continuum is set by the readout neuron's integration window and synaptic dynamics
interpretations: the productive empirical question is which downstream readouts each circuit deploys
asking whether spikes carry information beyond rates without specifying a readout is ill-posed
